title: [Jolteon] Battle Guide
summary: A 130 Speed [Electric] special attacker that defines the high-end Speed tier and uses [volt-switch] to hold tempo through long matches.
strengths: 130 Speed sits above almost every unboosted attacker
weaknesses: [Ground] type immunity walls the primary STAB outright
Thin 65/60/95 defensive line folds to priority

recommendedMoves.reason: Pivots out while still dealing damage

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Jolteon] is a pure [Electric] special attacker whose reputation is built on a 130 Speed tier paired with 110 Special Attack. In the Champions format that stat line is still above almost every unboosted threat, which keeps [Jolteon] relevant as a high-speed [pivot] and opening cleaner. The [volt-absorb] ability is a nice cherry on top — [Electric] attackers become free healing opportunities rather than threats, making [Jolteon] one of the few single-type Electrics that can safely [pivot] into [Electric] damage.

## Strengths
The Speed tier is still the pitch. [Jolteon] outpaces faster mega forms, choice-scarf users on lower-base threats, and every defensive Pokemon. [thunderbolt] delivers reliable STAB damage, and [volt-switch] turns every shaky matchup into a free [pivot], building tempo across a long match instead of trading tempo away. [thunder-wave] on the fourth slot lets [Jolteon] shut down the opposing Speed tier when a real [sweeper] is about to enter.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The [Ground] immunity is the structural headache. Coverage on [Jolteon] is narrow, since it learns few strong non-[Electric] special moves, so one safe switch-in for the opponent can invalidate the slot for several turns. The bulk is thin on the physical side at 65/60, so any priority move or strong physical attacker [revenge]-kills it easily.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is the locked [Electric] STAB. [volt-switch] is mandatory because it's the primary reason to run [Jolteon]. [shadow-ball] is the best coverage slot available, punishing [Ghost] and [Psychic] walls that would blank the [Electric] STAB. [thunder-wave] fills the fourth slot as a speed control tool for the team's cleaners. [hidden-power] [ice] is an alternate option for [Ground] coverage but loses damage floor overall.

## Item · Ability · Tera
choice-scarf is the default build because it pushes [Jolteon]'s already elite speed tier into one that answers every scarfed attacker in the metagame. [magnet] is an alternate for a more flexible build that trades speed for [Electric] damage. [focus-sash] fits the lead role and guarantees one clean attack against a faster special attacker. The ability must be [volt-absorb]. Tera [Ice] gives coverage for [Dragon], [Flying], and [Ground] attackers, while Tera [Electric] maxes STAB damage for [thunderbolt].

## Teammates & Synergy
The biggest structural requirement is a [Ground] answer. Slow [u-turn] partners extend the [volt-switch] [pivot] chain, which turns [Jolteon]'s coverage problem into a managed rotation rather than a dead slot. A physical breaker behind [Jolteon] cleans up walls that would otherwise [absorb] its output, and status-absorber teammates keep [Jolteon]'s [thunder-wave] plan from being flipped back.

## Play Patterns
[Jolteon] prefers to open the game. Drop [thunder-wave] on known setup sweepers that would otherwise outpace you, and use [shadow-ball] against [Ghost] pivots that try to swap in for free. Save the Tera for the moment a [Ground] or priority move is about to land, and always [volt-switch] out before committing to a locked coverage move against unknown walls.
